cluster: C5 — Close the launch surface + honest help (intake/feedback): --cap-add/--cap-drop identity cut, --privileged remap deletion, exhaustive flag table + honest-rejection list in docker-parity-contract.md, usage-errors-to-stderr, ps/list help truthfulness, --name grammar hardening

play_verdicts.play: Play 1 — Close the launch-flag surface: execute the '--cap paths: delete' cut, delete the --privileged remap, amend docker-parity-contract.md with an exhaustive flag table + honest-rejection list
play_verdicts.reason: The deletion IS justified by internal evidence, conditionally. Verified: (1) every other element of cut row hold-on-identity.md:187 is executed — src/access/invocation.c is 62 lines of sudo-provenance detection only, no grants/elevation; (2) the normative (non-draft) docker-parity-contract.md:16-27 flag list omits both cap flags and --privileged while declaring 'Tests assert the shapes written here' — tests/test_hold.sh:2517-2602 assert shapes the contract does not write; (3) zero user-facing documentation: `./hold --help | grep -ci cap` = 0, no help_action topic in src/cli.c mentions them, --privileged appears only in docs/archive/; (4) --privileged's remap (cli_main.c:136-139 → requested_system at 342-343) is archive-era elevation vocabulary that lies to Docker muscle memory and produces an rc-3 error never naming the flag typed (cli_main.c:731); (5) capability management is container-substrate territory the identity doc ('more than nohup, less than systemd') and the honest-rejection precedent (-p/-v, contract lines 32-34) both exclude. The replacement spelling for --privileged's actual effect already exists and is what every test uses: the leading --system flag (cli_main.c:531-535, pinned at test_hold.sh:2772 '--system list' and 4193) and plain sudo/root (root already defaults to the system store, making --privileged a literal no-op for root). Zero tests and zero current docs use --privileged. BUT the play's own sketch is internally contradictory: step 2 says 'delete the --privileged branch' while the verify step demands `hold --privileged /bin/true` print an honest message and exit 5 — plain deletion yields 'cannot start "--privileged": command not found' rc 1 via the PATH fallthrough (start.c:832-836). Rejection branches, not silent deletion, are the contract-consistent mechanism. Also the sketch's 'emit empty CapAdd/CapDrop arrays' is wrong: record.c:135-141 currently emits null when the counts are zero, so hardcoding null (not []) keeps every cap-less record byte-identical across the cut and keeps the test_hold.sh:2960 key pin green with zero test edits. And --restart-delay must NOT enter the Docker-borrowed table — docker run has no such flag; the table's own header ('behave exactly like their docker run counterparts') would become false. Owner-ratification safety valve is mandatory since hold-on-identity.md:3 is still 'draft for redline': docs commit lands first and separately so reversing the decision is a one-paragraph edit plus one git revert.
amendments: --privileged and --cap-add/--cap-drop (all four spellings: split and = forms) get explicit rejection branches mirroring reject_publish_option (cli_main.c:282-286): exit 5, stderr message that names the flag typed. --privileged's message must point at the real spelling: 'run as root/sudo for the root-managed store, or pass --system'. --cap-*'s message: capabilities are a container-substrate concept; run under a user with the rights you need.

Record writer keeps the Config keys but hardcodes '"CapAdd": null' / '"CapDrop": null' (record.c:135-141 already emits null for zero counts) — byte-identical records for all cap-less launches, test_hold.sh:2960 and the 3024 fixture stay green untouched. 'Privileged' stays as-is: record.c:143 derives it from uid==0, never from the flag.

--restart-delay is documented in a NEW 'Hold-native launch flags' subsection of docker-parity-contract.md, not the Docker-borrowed table. That subsection must also name -f/--tail and --console (cli_main.c:484-493) plus --quiet/--system/-- — otherwise the 'closed set' claim is false the day it lands (these leading flags are currently doc-absent everywhere current).

The deletion (code + the three tests) is its own revertable commit, separate from and after the docs commit; CHANGELOG/release notes must flag BOTH the cut of working functionality AND the redial semantics change: a pre-cut record carrying CapDrop:['ALL'] now redials with full capabilities (loader lines record.c:762-774 deleted), which is a silent privilege WIDENING of a sandboxing recipe. If the owner finds that unacceptable, the alternative is a one-line refusal on redial, but default to the release note per no-legacy-shims.

When marking hold-on-identity.md:187 executed, correct its '(src/access/)' parenthetical — the cap code actually lives in cli_main.c/start.c/record.c/call.c, not src/access/; the executed row must be accurate.

Delete the now-orphaned helpers with the machinery or the zero-warning build fails: append_string_option (cli_main.c:18-19 + body, cap-only caller), the entire #if __linux__ cap engine start.c:440-678 including HOLD_CAP_NAMES/cap_last_cap_value/normalize_cap_name/cap_name_to_value/cap_set_bit/cap_permitted_bit_is_set AND the non-Linux ENOTSUP stub (672-678), the forward decl (start.c:20), the includes <linux/capability.h>/<sys/syscall.h> (start.c:11,13; verified no other syscall() in start.c) and <sys/prctl.h> if no other prctl use survives (verified: none), struct fields types.h:20-23 and runtime.h:28-31, the privileged param/field (cli_main.c:16,49,117,136-139,342-343), threading at start.c:296-299/347-348/803-806/816-817/1078-1081/1139/1365-1372/1515-1516/1525-1526, call.c:149-152, record.c:14-15/21-22/28-29/292-300/762-774.

Keep the deletion scoped: do NOT fold in the dead non-docker_run banner branch (start.c:1478-1494) or the stale explicit_start_argv0 'start' compare — that is another cluster's subtraction (named deferral D6).

play_verdicts.play: Play 2 — Usage errors obey the stderr/nonzero discipline; help stops claiming 'ps is an alias'; list/ps honor -h; purge usage strings complete
play_verdicts.reason: Five of the six defects verified exactly as claimed: (1) `hold -d`/`hold --quiet` with no command hits cli_main.c:549-552, hold_usage() printf's ~1.7KB to stdout (commands.c:66), rc 5 — violating docker-parity-contract.md:66-69's normative 'stdout carries machine data only', and no test pins stdout-on-error (the only bare-hold pin is the rc-0 'PLACE A CALL' path at test_hold.sh:815-819, which is untouched); (2) commands.c:79 'the call table (ps is an alias)' and README.md:94 contradict docker-parity-contract.md:38-39, the dispatch comment at cli_main.c:712-714, and the pinned rc-1 rejection of ps scope flags (test_hold.sh:2807-2813) — no test pins the alias sentence; (3) `hold list -h`/`hold ps -h` die rc 1 'unknown flag' because the shared loop's dash rejection (cli_main.c:633-639) fires before the -h handler at 669, breaking commands.c:94's promise — while every other verb's -h works (pinned for end at test_hold.sh:836 and attach at test_040_dockerish.sh:96); (4) cli.c:42-45 purge-family usage strings omit -s/-u (and rm omits -a) that cli_main.c:610-616 accepts and cli.c:147 documents; (5) start.c:778/819 hold_usage()-to-stdout on validation failure. ONE claim is a misread: the 'unknown-command fallthrough' at cli_main.c:930-932 is UNREACHABLE — every name in command_specs (cli.c:25-49) canonicalizes to a dispatched verb (checked all 23 rows against the dispatch arms at 656-928), and non-spec tokens are never parser-owned, so they take the launch path and fail 'command not found' rc 1 in start.c:832-836 instead (that is what test_hold.sh:840-848 actually pins for `hold -l`/`hold --list`). Convert 930-932 defensively but the play may not claim it as a user-visible fix nor promise a test for it. Note also start.c's two hold_usage() sites are defensive: opts is never NULL from either caller, and argc<=0 is reachable only via a crafted record with empty argv on redial — still worth converting (a tampered record should not dump help to stdout) but it is hardening, not a live user flow.
amendments: Correct the finding: cli_main.c:930-932 is dead defensive code (all specs dispatched; unknown tokens go to the PATH-launch 'command not found' rc-1 path pinned at test_hold.sh:840-848). Convert it to the stderr one-liner for consistency, no test, and do not advertise it as a behavior fix.

Keep rc 5 on the flags-only path and rc 1 on list/ps unknown-flag rejection exactly as pinned; the exit-code-table contradiction (cli.c:100-107 documents usage=1 while tests pin rc 5 at test_hold.sh:4053-4067 and rc 1 at 820-825/2807-2813) is the named deferral D1 requiring an owner ruling — this commit changes channels and wording only, never codes.

Implement -h/--help for list/ps as an exemption in the dash-rejection at cli_main.c:633 so the token flows to cmd_argv and the existing generic handler at 669 fires (cmd_argc==1, !saw_owned_delimiter). Verify the edges: `hold list -- -h` must still treat -h as a (rejected-invalid) name filter via hold_valid_alias, and `hold list -h foo` falls to the arity rejection rc 5 — both acceptable.

Make all four purge-family spec rows (cli.c:42-45) byte-identical: 'usage: hold purge [<target>] [-a|--all] [-s|--system] [-u|--user] [--force]' — the only test pin is a prefix grep 'usage: hold purge' at test_hold.sh:4063, which stays green; rm gains [-a|--all] which cli_main.c:596 already accepts for it.
The stderr one-liner must be the existing house string 'usage: hold [flags] <cmd|id|name> [args...]' already used at cli_main.c:350/359/431 — do not invent a second phrasing.

Fix README.md:94 in the same commit as commands.c:79 so the two surfaces cannot drift apart again; new test asserts bare `hold` output contains a distinct 'hold ps' line and does not contain 'ps is an alias'.

play_verdicts.play: Cluster-mandate item missing from both plays — harden --name grammar (validate.c) to reject flag-look-alike names
play_verdicts.reason: Neither play's files_touched includes src/core/validate.c, yet the cluster mandate requires it and the finding is verified FACT: hold_valid_alias (validate.c:65-80) enforces only isalnum|'_'|'-' per character with no first-char rule, so '-d' and '_x' are valid call names; `hold -d --name -d sleep 5` succeeds because the launch-side validation (call.c:351, hold_generate_run_name_for_id) and rename (call.c:216) both delegate to it. docker-parity-contract.md:13-14 says borrowed flags 'behave exactly like their docker run counterparts' and Docker's --name grammar is [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]* — rejecting '-d' outright. The fix is one line (require isalnum on alias[0]) at the single choke point, and it automatically covers launch --name, rename, redial/action name resolution (resolve.c:158/199/258, call.c:43), the list/ps name filters (cli_main.c:818/840 — dash-leading already unreachable there due to the 633 flag rejection, but '_foo' filters change), and the record loader's name validation (record.c:619-621, 818). Verified zero pinned conflicts: no test uses a leading '-', '_', or '.' name (all test names are ps_live/docker-direct/ps-humanized-style, leading alphanumeric), and no test pins acceptance of flag-look-alike names. Side effect to release-note: a pre-existing record named '-d' or '_x' loses its name at load time (loader treats invalid Name as absent) — cosmetic, unpinned, and strictly safer than a name colliding with flag syntax.
amendments: Keep '.' rejected (stricter than Docker) and document the divergence explicitly in the contract's --name row ('names: leading alphanumeric, then [A-Za-z0-9_-]; stricter than Docker: no dot') — widening to accept '.' is surface growth deferred to redline (D5).

Pin both directions in the new test: rejection of '-d' and '_x' via --name (rc 5 'invalid call name') and via rename (rc 5), plus continued acceptance of interior-dash/underscore names (a-b_c) so the grammar cannot silently over-tighten.

pinned_conflicts.what: Asserts real CapEff change from --cap-drop ALL --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE under the root actor. DELETE with the code in the same commit.
Asserts Config.CapAdd==['NET_BIND_SERVICE'] and Config.CapDrop==['ALL'] project into `hold inspect`. DELETE with the code.

Asserts the restart worker re-applies capability metadata across restarts (start.c:347-348). DELETE with the code. Its incidental --restart-delay 0 usage is redundantly covered by 3503 and 3536-3540, which stay.

Pins CapAdd/CapDrop/Privileged as required Config KEYS (set membership only, values unchecked). KEPT GREEN with no test edit: writer hardcodes 'CapAdd': null / 'CapDrop': null (current empty-count output, record.c:137/141) and Privileged stays uid-derived (record.c:143).

Handcrafted record containing cap keys must still load without 'skipping corrupt record'. KEPT GREEN: the key-lookup JSON loader ignores unread keys (it already tolerates the fixture's unknown 'Origin' key); deleting the loader reads at record.c:762-774 leaves these keys inert.
Pin --restart-delay behavior (works with --restart; rc 5 with '--restart-delay requires --restart' when alone). UNCHANGED — the flag stays and gains contract documentation; do not touch these.

Pins bare `hold` → rc 0, 'PLACE A CALL' on STDOUT. Must stay green: the stderr conversion applies only to the argi>=argc rc-5 path (cli_main.c:549-552) and the --help/-h rc-0 path stays stdout (652-655).

Pins `hold -l` and `hold --list` → rc 1. These resolve via the PATH-launch 'command not found' path (start.c:832-836), NOT the flags-only path — untouched by the stderr fix. Any future idea of rejecting unknown leading flags as usage errors would collide with these pins; out of scope here.
Pins `hold ps -l/-s/-u` → rc 1, "unknown flag '<f>'" on stderr. The -h/--help exemption at cli_main.c:633 must not alter these three rejections.
Pins `hold purge -all` → rc 1 "unknown flag '-all'". Untouched by the purge usage-string change and the purge target[0]=='-' guard at cli_main.c:882-887.
Greps stderr for prefix 'usage: hold purge' on the arity error. The amended spec string keeps that prefix, so no test edit — but any rewording that drops the prefix breaks it.
Pin `hold end` rc 5 (arity path already stderr via cli.c:206-207), `hold end -h` help, and `hold attach -h` usage text — all untouched; regression-guard them in the verify run.

Elevation-era fixture fields flagged cut-blocked by the Test Auditor finding. NOT in this cluster's commits — read-side tolerance only; folding it in would balloon the deletion commit. Leave with a comment or defer.

implementation_risks: start.c merge collision with the console-broker cluster (critical broker.c:293 finding): both change perform_start_with_metadata_name_options_internal — C5 deletes cap threading at 803-806/816-817/1078-1081/1139/1365-1372/1515-1526 while the broker fix touches the console branch and target_pid_pipe plumbing (981/1017/1293-1295) in the same function. Land C5's pure subtraction first (it shrinks the function), then the broker fix rebases trivially; the reverse order forces a manual conflict resolve inside a security-sensitive launch path.

Silent privilege widening on redial of pre-cut records: a record whose recipe carries CapDrop:['ALL'] (a sandboxing restriction) redials with FULL capabilities once the loader reads (record.c:762-774) are deleted. Mitigation is mandatory: CHANGELOG/release-notes entry naming this exact semantics change, with the optional one-line refusal offered to the owner. Do not ship the deletion commit without it.

Zero-warning build breakage from orphaned code: after deleting apply_child_capabilities, the statics normalize_cap_name/cap_name_to_value/cap_set_bit/cap_permitted_bit_is_set/cap_last_cap_value/HOLD_CAP_NAMES, the forward decl at start.c:20, append_string_option in cli_main.c (cap-only caller), and the includes <linux/capability.h>/<sys/syscall.h>/<sys/prctl.h> (verified: no other syscall()/prctl() in start.c, none elsewhere in src/) all become -Wunused/-Wunused-function failures if missed. Delete the non-Linux ENOTSUP stub (start.c:672-678) too or the #else branch dangles.

Record-shape regression: accidentally deleting the CapAdd/CapDrop/Privileged KEYS from the writer (instead of hardcoding null/null/uid-derived) fails test_hold.sh:2960 and changes record bytes for every launch. The amendment (hardcode null) makes cap-less records byte-identical pre/post cut — diff a before/after record in review to prove it.

--privileged rejection vs deletion ambiguity: plain deletion routes `hold --privileged cmd` to 'cannot start "--privileged": command not found' rc 1 — a worse message than today's rc 3. The rejection branch (exit 5, names the flag, points at sudo/--system) must land in the same commit as the branch deletion, and the new rejection tests must cover both the bare and = spellings of --cap-add/--cap-drop plus --privileged.

hold_valid_alias hardening ripples beyond intake: record loader (record.c:619-621) and public-index reader (record.c:818) silently drop pre-existing '-'/'_'-leading names (NAMES cell empties, name resolution stops matching). Unpinned and safer, but user-visible — release-note it. Also confirm generated adjective_noun names and every test-suite name (all leading-alphanumeric, verified) pass the new rule before landing.

The -h exemption at cli_main.c:633 must be scoped to !literal_owned_arg so `hold list -- -h` keeps treating -h as a literal (invalid) name filter; getting the delimiter interaction wrong would reopen the exact 'flag misread as name' hole the rejection exists to close.

Contract-table falsity risk: if the 'exhaustive accepted-flag table' omits the live leading flags -f/--tail and --console (cli_main.c:484-493) or misfiles --restart-delay under the Docker-borrowed section, the amended contract is wrong on day one and the closed-set enforcement idea (rejection tests catching fallthrough) loses its teeth.

Script-compat break (sanctioned): any script that parsed the stdout help from a flags-only invocation (`id=$(hold -d "$CMD")` with empty CMD) now gets empty stdout + stderr usage at rc 5 — this is the contract-mandated fix but belongs in the CHANGELOG under behavior changes.

Draft-doc authority risk: hold-on-identity.md:3 is still 'draft for redline'; if the owner redlines the other way (promote cap flags into the contract), only commit 1 (docs) and commit 2 (deletion) being separate keeps the reversal a one-paragraph edit plus one git revert. Squashing them destroys the safety valve this cluster's acceptance is conditioned on.
revised_change_set: Four commits, in this order, on tempo-refinement. COMMIT 1 (docs only — the redline decision, trivially revertable): docker-parity-contract.md — (a) keep the 9-flag Docker-borrowed table (lines 16-27) and add a --name grammar note ('leading alphanumeric, then [A-Za-z0-9_-]; stricter than Docker: no dot'); (b) add a 'Hold-native launch flags' subsection: --restart-delay <seconds> (requires --restart), -f/--tail, --console, --quiet, --system, -- (end of flags); (c) extend the honest-rejection paragraph (lines 32-34) to name --privileged ('not a hold concept: run as root/sudo for the root-managed store, or pass --system') and --cap-add/--cap-drop ('capabilities are a container-substrate concept; run under a user with the rights you need'), stating rejections exit 5 with the flag named on stderr; (d) state the closed-set rule: a bare-form flag is either in these tables or rejected. hold-on-identity.md — mark the line-187 cut row's '--cap paths' executed and correct its '(src/access/)' misattribution (the code lived in cli_main.c/start.c/record.c/call.c). COMMIT 2 (the identity cut — its own revertable commit): src/cli_main.c: delete parse_docker_run_flag's privileged param+branch (16, 49, 113-117, 136-139) and the remap (342-343); replace the four --cap parse branches (397-428) with reject_caps_option(flag_name) and add reject_privileged_option(), both mirroring reject_publish_option (282-286), matching bare and = spellings, exit 5; delete append_string_option (18-19 + body) and the cap fields from struct cli_run_options (58-61) and start_options_from_run (99-101). src/runtime/start.c: delete the cap engine block 440-678 (both #if arms), forward decl (20), now-unused includes <linux/capability.h>, <sys/syscall.h>, <sys/prctl.h> (11-13), the restart-worker params+call (296-299, 347-348), locals/validation (803-806, 816-817), thread-through (1078-1081), launch call (1139), recipe copies (1365-1372), frees (1515-1516, 1525-1526). src/runtime/call.c: delete recipe cap replay (149-152). src/store/record.c: writer 135-141 becomes hardcoded '\"CapAdd\": null,' / '\"CapDrop\": null,' (byte-identical to current cap-less output; Privileged at 143 untouched); delete frees/inits (14-15, 21-22, 28-29), legacy top-level writes (292-300), loader reads (762-774). include/hold/types.h: delete recipe fields 20-23; include/hold/runtime.h: delete 28-31. tests/test_hold.sh: delete functions at 2517-2537, 2539-2560, 2562-2602 and registrations 4213-4215; add test_docker_cap_and_privileged_rejected next to test_docker_publish_and_volume_rejected. CHANGELOG: flag the cut for owner ratification + the pre-cut-record redial semantics (CapDrop recipes now redial unconstrained). Verify: make (zero warnings, static + dynamic), scripts/lint_layers.sh, full test suite, before/after byte-diff of a cap-less record, manual probes of all five rejected spellings. COMMIT 3 (channel + help honesty): src/cli_main.c: 549-552 → fprintf(stderr, \"usage: hold [flags] <cmd|id|name> [args...]\\n\"), keep rc 5; same one-liner at the dead-defensive 930-932 (keep rc 1, no test — unreachable, all command_specs dispatch); exempt -h/--help from the list/ps dash rejection at 633 so the existing 669 handler serves help rc 0 (scoped to !literal_owned_arg). src/runtime/start.c: 777-780 and 818-820 hold_usage() → same stderr one-liner (defensive; reachable only via crafted empty-argv record). src/runtime/commands.c:79: split into 'hold list — your scoped ledger (live and past)' + new 'hold ps — Docker's machine-wide running view' line. README.md:94: same rewording. src/cli.c:42-45: all four purge-family usage strings identical: 'usage: hold purge [<target>] [-a|--all] [-s|--system] [-u|--user] [--force]' (prefix preserved for the 4063 pin). Tests: add test_usage_error_channels + test_list_ps_help_flags + bare-hold no-alias assertion. COMMIT 4 (name grammar): src/core/validate.c hold_valid_alias (65-80): require isalnum on alias[0]; add test_name_grammar_rejects_flag_lookalikes. CHANGELOG note: legacy '-'/'_'-leading record names render unnamed. NAMED DEFERRALS (not in this cluster's commits): D1 exit-code table unification (cli.c:100-107 vs rc-5/rc-1 test pins — owner ruling); D2 console.md/store.md deleted-subsystem refresh (console.md:52,96); D3 logs -t/--time/--date identity overpromise (hold-on-identity.md:63-66); D4 shell/console alias fate (cli.c:30,41); D5 accepting '.' in names (full Docker grammar); D6 dead non-docker_run banner branch + stale 'start' compare in start.c (1478-1494, 381) and the elevation-era public-index fixture fields (test_hold.sh:2492-2500).
test_plan: NEW test_docker_cap_and_privileged_rejected (register beside 'Docker publish and volume flags are rejected', run_test line ~4126): for each of --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE, --cap-add=NET_BIND_SERVICE, --cap-drop ALL, --cap-drop=ALL, --privileged prepended to '/bin/true': set +e; out=$("$HOLD_BIN" $flag /bin/true 2>"$TEST_ROOT/rej.err"); rc=$?; set -e; assert rc -eq 5, [ -z "$out" ] (stdout empty), and stderr names the flag; for --privileged additionally grep for the --system/sudo pointer.

NEW test_usage_error_channels: printf '' | "$HOLD_BIN" -d >out 2>err → rc 5, out empty, err matches '^usage: hold \[flags\]'; "$HOLD_BIN" --quiet → same shape; re-assert bare "$HOLD_BIN" rc 0 with 'PLACE A CALL' on stdout so the deliberate help path is pinned against the same edit (complements existing 815-819).

NEW test_list_ps_help_flags: "$HOLD_BIN" list -h → rc 0, stdout mentions 'scoped ledger'; "$HOLD_BIN" ps -h → rc 0, stdout mentions 'machine-wide'; "$HOLD_BIN" list --help → rc 0; and "$HOLD_BIN" list -- -h → nonzero with 'invalid name' (delimiter keeps -h literal); leave the 2807-2813 ps -l/-s/-u rc-1 pins untouched as the counterpart.
NEW assertion in test_argument_edges (or a new test): "$HOLD_BIN" | grep -q 'hold ps' and ! grep -q 'ps is an alias' — pins the corrected top-level help alongside the existing 'PLACE A CALL' pin.

NEW test_name_grammar_rejects_flag_lookalikes: "$HOLD_BIN" -d --name -d sleep 1 → rc 5 stderr 'invalid call name'; "$HOLD_BIN" -d --name _x /bin/true → rc 5; start a call, "$HOLD_BIN" rename <id> -d → rc 5; positive control: "$HOLD_BIN" -d --name a-b_c /bin/sh -c 'sleep 0.2' succeeds and 'hold list a-b_c' finds it (isolated store via the harness HOME/TEST_ROOT as in every existing test).

DELETE tests: test_root_capability_drop_all_then_add_preserves_added_cap (2517-2537, registration 4213), test_direct_capability_metadata_projects_to_inspect (2539-2560, registration 4214), test_restart_worker_applies_capability_metadata (2562-2602, registration 4215) — in the SAME commit as the code cut, never before.

MUST-STAY-GREEN (no edits, run as the regression gate after every commit): 2960-2974 record Config-key pin; 3013-3050 Docker-shaped fallback reader fixture; 3503 + 3536-3540 --restart-delay pins; 815-848 argument edges (bare hold, purge -all rc 1, end rc 5, -l/--list rc 1); 2807-2813 ps flag rejections; 4053-4067 arity rc-5 usage pins (incl. the 'usage: hold purge' prefix grep at 4063); test_040_dockerish.sh:96 attach -h.

Byte-level record check (manual, in the deletion commit's verification): start `hold -d true` on the pre-cut and post-cut binaries in isolated stores and diff the two records modulo Id/timestamps/pids — Config must be identical including 'CapAdd': null / 'CapDrop': null / 'Privileged': false.

Full gate per commit: make (zero warnings, static + dynamic targets), scripts/lint_layers.sh clean, bash tests/test_hold.sh 135-minus-3-plus-new all passing, tests/test_040_dockerish.sh passing; on the deletion commit also run the root lane (HOLD_REQUIRE_ROOT_TESTS=1 where available) since the deleted tests were root-actor tests and the remaining root tests must still pass without them.
